title: haplopappus phyllocephalus subsp. annuus
intro: Haplopappus phyllocephalus subsp. annuus, also known as the annual hop-skip-grass, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). Native to the southwestern United States, this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Haplopappus phyllocephalus subsp. annuus include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to treat a range of ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
